attributes.description: Alerts when `postgresql.temp.io > 100 MB` in the window. Queries spilling to disk due to insufficient work_mem.
attributes.name: [PostgreSQL OTel] High temp I/O volume

investigation_guide.blob:
## PostgreSQL high temp I/O volume

### What fired
Temp bytes written exceeded 100 MB during the window.

### Why it matters
Temp files mean sort or hash operations exceeded `work_mem` and spilled to disk. Slower by orders of magnitude.

### Triage
1. Identify the slow queries spilling.

### Remediation
- Raise `work_mem` for sessions/roles.
- Rewrite queries to reduce memory need.

### Tuning
- 100 MB threshold.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-postgresqlreceiver.otel-*
// Temp I/O (bytes written to temp files) indicates memory-constrained workloads
| WHERE postgresql.temp.io IS NOT NULL
| EVAL temp_io_val = TO_DOUBLE(postgresql.temp.io)
| WHERE temp_io_val > 0
| STATS temp_io_bytes = SUM(temp_io_val) BY service.instance.id, postgresql.database.name
// Threshold: 100 MB in window; adjust for your scrape interval and expected load
| WHERE temp_io_bytes > 104857600
